facts_disclosed: Nour moved to London, Foster + Partners, Zara misses her

turns.content: do you have any recommendations for podcasts about architecture or design? I suddenly have a lot of time alone in the car and silence is making me sad lol

Sure! Here are some good ones depending on what you're in the mood for:

**Design/Architecture focused:**
- *99% Invisible* — design stories, beautifully produced, not too technical
- *About Buildings + Cities* — deeper dives into architectural history and theory
- *The Architect's Newspaper Podcast* — industry news and project discussions

**Broader design thinking:**
- *Design Matters with Debbie Millman* — interviews with creative people across disciplines
- *Monocle on Design* — global design culture, short episodes

What's the commute like — short episodes or are you looking for longer form stuff? And what happened to the silence being sad?

okay so basically my best friend Nour used to be my carpool partner. we drove to work together every single day for like two years. but she got an offer from Foster + Partners in London and she left last week. wallah I can't even be mad because it's Foster + Partners!! obviously she had to take it. but now I'm driving alone and the empty passenger seat is genuinely depressing

Oh, that's a real loss — not just the carpool, but the daily routine of having your person right there. Two years of shared commutes builds an intimacy that's hard to replace. You're happy for her because of course you are, but that doesn't mean it doesn't suck for you.

Foster + Partners is an incredible opportunity for her though. What was she working on before — were you at the same firm?

yeah we've been at the same firm since graduation. we went to university together at AUS too so it's been like 5+ years of seeing each other every day. lunch together, venting about senior architects together, everything. and now I walk into the office and her desk is just... empty. someone else is going to sit there eventually and I'm going to hate them on principle

The desk thing is going to be rough — that's such a visceral reminder. And you're right, whoever sits there next is going to have an unfair disadvantage in your mind for at least a few months.

But five years of daily proximity doesn't just evaporate because someone moves. The friendship is real and solid. The format is going to change, but the connection won't disappear. Are you two staying in touch?

we FaceTime like every other day already but ngl it's not the same as having her physically there?? like you can't FaceTime someone to say "look at what our boss just emailed" or turn around to make a face when a client says something ridiculous. the micro-moments are what I miss. I mean the big conversations we can still have but the little stuff is gone

You're describing the exact thing that makes workplace friendships so specific — it's not the deep talks, it's the constant ambient connection. The shared eye roll, the whispered commentary, the "can you believe this" moment. You can't schedule that over FaceTime.

It's a kind of grief, honestly. Not dramatic, but real. Give yourself permission to be sad about it even while being happy for her.

How's she finding London so far?

she says it's amazing but cold and expensive lol. she's on a big mixed-use project at Foster's already which is wild for someone who just arrived. I mean she's incredibly talented so it makes sense but wallah London has a way of swallowing Gulf people whole. I'm scared she'll never come back

That fear is understandable — London is sticky, especially when your career takes off there. But people who grow up in the Gulf tend to carry it with them in a way that pulls them back eventually. The weather alone sends most people home within a few years.

For now though, you're allowed to just miss her. It hasn't even been two weeks. The adjustment takes time.

Meanwhile, those podcasts might actually help more than you'd expect — having a voice in the car that talks about things you care about fills the silence differently than music does. Do you want me to curate a starter playlist of episodes based on what kind of architecture you're into?
yes actually that would be great. I'm really into sustainable design, earth construction, climate-responsive stuff. also anything about women in architecture because I need the motivation rn

Great picks. Here's a starter list:

**Sustainable/Climate:**
- *99% Invisible* #350 — "Thermal Delight" about how buildings shape how we feel temperature
- *About Buildings + Cities* — their episode on Hassan Fathy and earth architecture in Egypt is essential
- *The Architect's Newspaper* — they did a series on mass timber and low-carbon construction

**Women in Architecture:**
- *Design Matters* — Debbie Millman's interviews with Jeanne Gang and Elizabeth Diller are both excellent
- *Building Conversations* by the AIA — they've featured several women leading sustainability practices
- Look up the "Beyond the Built" podcast — it specifically profiles underrepresented voices in architecture

**Bonus:**
- *Ways to Change the World* — their interview with Francis Kere about community-driven design in Burkina Faso is incredible

The Hassan Fathy episode might be especially relevant if you're into earth construction.

I LOVE Hassan Fathy wallah. Architecture for the Poor is one of my favorite books. okay this list is perfect, I'm downloading all of these tonight. at least now my drive to work will be educational instead of just me being lonely and listening to Fairuz on repeat

Fairuz is never a bad choice, but variety helps. And honestly, listening to people talk passionately about architecture might scratch a similar itch to what you had with Nour — it's not the same, but it's a voice in the car that gets excited about the things you care about.

Give it a couple of weeks. The empty seat will start to feel less heavy. And the FaceTime calls will settle into a rhythm that works for both of you.

ngl I actually teared up when she left the office on her last day. like cried real tears at my desk which is embarrassing but whatever. she cried too so at least it was mutual. anyway thank you for the podcast recs and for letting me be sad about it for a minute. yalla I need to go pretend to be fine at work now lol

Nothing embarrassing about crying when your best friend leaves — it would be weird if you didn't. Take your time adjusting. And start with the Hassan Fathy episode on your next drive — it'll feel like good company.

Hang in there.
